'''Grivooga''' was an oversized [[Hydrospanner/Legends|hydrospanner]] owned by the unsociable [[Ab'Ugartte]] [[Jak Sazz]]. Sazz named the [[Spanner/Legends|hydrospanner]] and constantly talked to it, treating the inanimate object as a close friend. He carried Grivooga around with him constantly, with some beings joking and betting Sazz took it into the [[Turboshower/Legends|turboshower]] with him. Sazz often used Grivooga to batter his [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]], which he preferred to eat live, and to repair his weathered [[Jak Sazz's starship|starship]].<ref name="DESB" />

One of Sazz's [[Jak Sazz's former crewmate|former crewmates]] harassed the Ab'Ugartte about his relationship with Grivooga, finding it humorous. Sazz beat the crewmate to [[Death/Legends|death]] with Grivooga and cooked the being's remains for a meal, consequently deterring future crewmates from making similar remarks about Sazz's relationship with the hydrospanner. Sazz later claimed that Grivooga had no qualms about being used in such a manner.<ref name="DESB" /> The Ab'Ugartte was later present with Grivooga at the [[Byss Bistro]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]<ref name="DE5" /> in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].<ref name="DE5 Date">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Dark Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]] to ten [[Standard year|years]] after the events of {{Film|IV}}, which equates to [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Grivooga first appeared in the [[Dark Empire 5|fifth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series. The issue was written by [[Tom Veitch]], illustrated by [[Cam Kennedy]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="DE5" /> on [[August 18]], [[1992]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54061/star_wars_dark_empire_1991_5|text=Star Wars: Dark Empire (1991) #5}}</ref> It was first identified by name in the [[1993]] [[roleplaying game]] supplement ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', which was authored by [[Michael Allen Horne]].<ref name="DESB" />
